core_mechanism: dead-reckoning / path integration: propagating a state estimate by cumulatively integrating cheap incremental relative-change measurements, whose integration drift is periodically reset by sparse, expensive absolute reference fixes (a predictor–corrector scheme trading frequent relative updates against infrequent absolute anchoring)
brief_reason: The method mainly integrates low-cost accelerometer/compass (relative motion/heading) readings to track position and only invokes the expensive GPS infrequently 'for synchronization', i.e. to correct the drift accumulated by integrating relative measurements.
